title: Money and consumer habits
explanation:
Money and consumer habits

  • budget, savings, debt, bargain, subscription
  • spend, waste, compare, charge
  • phrases: live within your means, cut back on

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: We need a monthly ____ so we know where our money goes. | *budget | receipt | platform
He is trying to ____ on eating out to save money. | *cut back | take off | set out
A free trial turns into a paid ____ after thirty days. | *subscription | vacancy | campaign
She found a real ____ in the sale. | *bargain | schedule | shelter
questions.feedback: Bargain = something good at a low price.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: If you borrow too much, you can end up in ____.  | debt
I always ____ prices online before buying electronics. | compare
They put some money into their ____ every month. | savings
Some shops ____ extra for delivery. | charge
Families often ____ money on things they do not need. | waste

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: It is important to live within your ____.  | *means | shift | evidence
Her main source of ____ is freelance design work. | *income | debt | budget
They need to keep track of every regular monthly ____. | *payment | platform | habitat
He is trying to become a more responsible ____. | *consumer | witness | editor
